_metadata.purpose: DRAFT authored exemplars for Path C interaction-rule fields
_metadata.authoring_rule: Every sub-rule must pass scripts/validate_safety_copy.py --strict before merge into scripts/data/ingredient_interaction_rules.json
review_checklist: alert_headline reads as a calm headline, not a command
alert_body uses conditional framing (if you take, talk to, etc.) for severe rules
informational_note never uses imperative verbs (stop, avoid, do not)
Copy matches the cited mechanism — no claims beyond sources

drafts.alert_body: Berberine lowers blood sugar with effects comparable to metformin. If you take a diabetes medication, monitor glucose closely and talk to your prescriber before adding berberine.
drafts.informational_note: Berberine has blood-sugar-lowering effects relevant to people on diabetes medications.
drafts.authoring_notes: Canonical example from user conversation. Old copy read as a clinician directive — new headline is gentle, body is conditional, note is neutral for users with no matching profile.

drafts.alert_body: Berberine has shown MAO-inhibiting activity. If you take an MAOI antidepressant, talk to your prescriber before adding berberine.
drafts.informational_note: Berberine has theoretical MAO-inhibiting activity relevant to people on MAOI antidepressants.
drafts.authoring_notes: MAO inhibition in berberine is in-vitro only — 'theoretical' is a more accurate framing than 'established clinical risk'.

drafts.alert_headline: Do not combine with transplant medications
drafts.alert_body: St Johns Wort lowers blood levels of immunosuppressants like cyclosporine and tacrolimus, risking transplant rejection. If you take these medications, do not combine.
drafts.informational_note: St Johns Wort strongly affects drug metabolism — relevant to people on transplant or immunosuppressant medications.
drafts.authoring_notes: Contraindicated severity — directive body is acceptable (transplant rejection is severe). Note preserves conditional framing.

drafts.alert_headline: Do not combine with MAOI antidepressants
drafts.alert_body: 5-HTP raises serotonin levels directly. If you take an MAOI antidepressant, combining with 5-HTP carries serotonin syndrome risk. Do not combine.
drafts.informational_note: 5-HTP raises serotonin — relevant to people on MAOI or SSRI antidepressants.
drafts.authoring_notes: Classic contraindicated pair. Body is directive; informational_note preserves neutral framing.

drafts.alert_body: Ephedra carries serious cardiovascular and neurologic risks and is FDA-banned as a supplement ingredient. If you are pregnant, do not use ephedra-containing products.
drafts.informational_note: Ephedra is an FDA-banned ingredient with cardiovascular risks relevant to anyone pregnant or planning pregnancy.
drafts.authoring_notes: Substance banned + pregnancy rule — both directive. informational_note preserves conditional framing.

drafts.alert_body: Vitamin K directly antagonizes warfarin. If you take warfarin, changes in vitamin K intake can destabilize your INR — talk to your prescriber before adding or stopping.
drafts.informational_note: Vitamin K can change how warfarin works — relevant to people on warfarin or related blood thinners.
drafts.authoring_notes: Classic warfarin-diet interaction. Body uses 'talk to your prescriber' — the actually-correct clinical guidance.
